user.title: Visionect Joan Server
user.description: Verbindungsdaten für Ihren Visionect-Server. Benutzername/Passwort oder API-Schlüssel/Geheimnis möglich.

menu.title: Visionect Joan Einstellungen
menu.description: Bereich wählen — jeder hat eine eigene Seite (nichts vermischt).

recovery_page.title: Standard-URL
recovery_page.description:
1. **Absenden** klicken, um **Ihre Standard-URL** zu erzeugen, dann hierher zurück. Token-Feld leer lassen und Formular speichern — es wird ein Geheimnis erzeugt. Bei Leck: neues Token und VSS aktualisieren.

2. Öffnen Sie die VSS-**Einstellungen** (**{vss_settings_url}**) → **HTML Backend** — die unten **generierte** Adresse in **Default URL** einfügen und **speichern**. Die Integration kann globale VSS-Einstellungen nicht selbst speichern, **daher ist dieser Schritt nötig**.

**Ihre Standard-URL:**
{recovery_url}

views_menu.title: Ansichten
views_menu.description: Namen für häufige URLs (z. B. Dashboards). In Diensten wählen Sie z. B. Wohnzimmer statt langer http-Adresse.

cache_cleanup.title: Cache & Speicher
cache_cleanup.description: Die Integration speichert temporäre Bilder und HTML. Hier steuern Sie Dateialter und Aufräum-Intervall.

data.cleanup_max_age_hours: Max. Dateialter (Stunden) — ältere Dateien werden gelöscht

tablet_language.title: Tablet-Sprache
tablet_language.description: Sprache für Joan-Tablet-Seiten und dauerhafte Home-Assistant-Benachrichtigungen (Akku, Verbindungsverlust, Safe Config, Diagnose). Auto = Sprache der HA-Oberfläche.

data_description.tablet_language: Nach Änderung Dienst visionect_joan.clear_web_cache mit restart_session ausführen, falls das Tablet noch alten Text zeigt.

battery_alerts.title: Akku-Benachrichtigungen
battery_alerts.description: Schwelle niedrig (5 / 10 / 15 %) und getrennte Schalter: Tablet, HA bei niedrigem Akku, HA bei vollem Akku.

data.battery_ha_low_notification_enabled: Home Assistant: Benachrichtigung bei niedrigem Akku
data.battery_ha_full_notification_enabled: Home Assistant: Benachrichtigung bei vollem Akku (Laden)

data_description.battery_low_threshold_pct: Warnung bei diesem Stand oder darunter (nur wenn unten aktiviert).
data_description.battery_tablet_alert_enabled: Ein: Niedrig-Akku-Bildschirm auf dem Joan bis Erholung oder Laden. Aus: kein Tablet-Hinweis.
data_description.battery_ha_low_notification_enabled: Ein: dauerhafte HA-Benachrichtigung bei niedrigem Akku. Aus: keine.
data_description.battery_ha_full_notification_enabled: Ein: Benachrichtigung bei vollem Akku beim Laden. Aus: keine.

ollama.title: Ollama Log-Analyse
ollama.description: Optional: Visionect-Kontext periodisch an Ollama senden. Formulierung folgt der Tablet-Sprache oben (Auto = HA-Oberflächensprache).

data.ollama_interval_min: Analyseintervall (Minuten) — min. 2; nach Änderung ggf. Integration neu laden

error.name_exists: Eine Ansicht mit diesem Namen existiert bereits.

sleep_schedule.name: Zyklischer Schlafplan (Minuten)
sleep_schedule.description: 0 = Push-Modus (Werk): Tablet schläft, wacht sofort bei neuem Inhalt (Push=EIN). 1–1440 = zyklisch alle X Min + Push. ERFORDERLICH: Sleep Manager in VSS.

set_url.name: Set Custom URL
set_url.description: Permanently changes the website or image displayed on the device screen. The set URL becomes the new Main view that the device will default to.

url.name: URL Address
url.description: Enter the full URL (starting with http:// or https://) you want to display. This can be a Home Assistant dashboard or any other website.

predefined_url.name: Select Predefined View
predefined_url.description: Choose one of the views you previously configured in the Integration Options. Allows for quick switching between favorite screens.

send_text.name: Send Text Message
send_text.description: Sends formatted text with an optional image to the device screen. This is a temporary view, perfect for notifications. Supports Jinja2 templates.

message.name: Message Content
message.description: Enter the text to display. You can use Jinja2 templates to dynamically insert data from Home Assistant, for example sensor states.

layout.name: Content Layout
layout.description: Choose how the image should be positioned relative to the text (e.g., image above text, beside text, etc.).

image_url.name: Image URL (Optional)
image_url.description: Link to an icon or photo to appear next to the text. For local HA files use format: http://HA_IP:8123/local/icon.png (upload file to //HA_IP/config/www/).

image_zoom.name: Image Zoom (%)
image_zoom.description: Adjust image size. 100% is original size, smaller values shrink, larger values enlarge the graphic.

text_color.name: Text Color
text_color.description: Select font color. E-ink screens typically support only Black and White.

background_color.name: Background Color
background_color.description: Select screen background color for this message. Usually White.

text_size.name: Font Size (px)
text_size.description: Define font size in pixels. Higher values mean larger and clearer text.

text_align.name: Text Alignment
text_align.description: Set text alignment: Left, Center, or Right.

font_family.name: Font Family
font_family.description: Choose a font style. Available fonts are optimized for readability on e-paper screens.

font_weight.name: Font Weight
font_weight.description: Choose whether text should be Bold or Normal.

small_screen_optimized.name: Optimization for Joan 6"
small_screen_optimized.description: Check this if using a 6-inch device (e.g., Joan 6). Adjusts margins and sizes to better fit content on screen.

action_webhook_id.name: Webhook ID (Right Button) (→)
action_webhook_id.description: ID of the webhook triggered when pressing the right button (arrow). Automatically detects internal address.

action_webhook_2_id.name: Webhook ID (Center Button) (✓)
action_webhook_2_id.description: ID of the webhook triggered when pressing the center button (check).

click_anywhere_to_action.name: Click Triggers Action
click_anywhere_to_action.description: If checked, hides other buttons. Touching ANY part of the screen will trigger the Right Button Webhook (→).

add_back_button.name: Show Back Button on Left (←)
add_back_button.description: Adds an exit/back button. For each device, configure the target in Views Configuration (Visionect Joan). To manage views: Settings, Devices and Services, Visionect Joan, Configure. Select Views menu.

click_anywhere_to_return.name: Click Triggers Back
click_anywhere_to_return.description: If checked, hides other buttons. Touching ANY part of the screen will trigger the Left Back Button (←).

back_button_url.name: Override Back Button Target
back_button_url.description: Highest priority. If you provide a URL or View Name here, the Back Button (←) will navigate there.

auto_return_seconds.name: Auto Return Back (seconds)
auto_return_seconds.description: Automatically closes the panel after time. Returns to the return target configured in device options.

set_display_rotation.name: Set Display Rotation
set_display_rotation.description: Changes the physical display orientation (Portrait/Landscape). Note: The device will reboot to apply the change, which may take a moment.

display_rotation.name: Rotation Angle
display_rotation.description: Select the desired screen rotation angle (0, 90, 180, or 270 degrees).

force_refresh.name: Force Screen Refresh
force_refresh.description: Sends a command to the device to wake up immediately (if possible) and fetch the latest image from the server. Useful when displayed content is stale.

clear_display.name: Clear Display (Flash)
clear_display.description: Forces a full screen refresh by flashing white/black. Used to remove ghosting artifacts that may appear on e-ink screens after long use.

sleep_device.name: Sleep Device
sleep_device.description: Forces the device to disconnect from Wi-Fi and enter deep sleep mode to save battery.

duration.name: Sleep Duration (seconds)
duration.description: Specify how long the device should sleep. After this time, it will wake up again to check for new tasks.

wake_device.name: Wake Device
wake_device.description: Attempts to wake up the device immediately. Works reliably only if the device is plugged in or configured in frequent listener mode.

send_qr_code.name: Send QR Code
send_qr_code.description: Generates a QR code from provided text (e.g., link, WiFi password) and displays it on the device.

data.name: Data to Encode
data.description: Text, URL, or WiFi config string to be converted into a QR code.

message.name: Text Message
message.description: Additional text displayed above or below the QR code, e.g., Scan to join WiFi.

qr_message_text_size.name: Text Size
qr_message_text_size.description: Font size for the text message.

qr_message_position.name: Message Position
qr_message_position.description: Choose whether text appears above or below the QR code.

box_size.name: QR Module Size
box_size.description: Determines the size of individual squares in the QR code. Increase for a physically larger code.

border.name: Margin (Border)
border.description: Thickness of the white border around the QR code.

fill_color.name: Code Color
fill_color.description: Color of the QR code elements (usually Black).

back_color.name: Background Color
back_color.description: Background color behind the QR code (usually White).

small_screen_optimized.name: Optimization for Joan 6"
small_screen_optimized.description: Check this if using a 6-inch device (e.g., Joan 6).

action_webhook_id.description: ID of the webhook triggered when pressing the right button (arrow).

action_webhook_2_id.name: Webhook ID (Center Button) (✓)
action_webhook_2_id.description: ID of the webhook triggered when pressing the center button (check).

auto_return_seconds.description: Automatically closes the panel after time. Returns to the return target configured in device options.

send_calendar.name: Send Calendar
send_calendar.description: Fetches events from a selected Home Assistant calendar and displays them as a list or monthly grid.

calendar_entity.name: Calendar Entity
calendar_entity.description: Select the calendar entity to source events from.

duration_days.name: Day Range
duration_days.description: How many upcoming days to fetch events for (applies to list view).

display_style.name: Display Style
display_style.description: Choose look: Modern List, Minimalist List, Full Monthly Grid, or Weather Calendar.

weather_entity.name: Weather Entity (Weather Calendar only)
weather_entity.description: Only used with 'Weather Calendar' display style. Select a weather entity to show a 5-day forecast with weather icons.

daily_production_entity.name: Daily Production

daily_grid_import_entity.name: Grid Import (Today)
daily_grid_import_entity.description: Amount of energy drawn from the grid.

daily_grid_export_entity.name: Grid Export (Today)
daily_grid_export_entity.description: Amount of energy sent to the grid.

image_zoom.name: Zoom (%)
image_zoom.description: Image scaling. 100% is original. Use e.g., 150% to zoom in.

display_rotation.name: Image Rotation

seconds_per_slide.name: Slide Duration (s)
seconds_per_slide.description: How often to change screen. Warning: Very frequent changes (e.g., every 60s) can drain battery quickly!

loop.name: Loop Slideshow
loop.description: Return to first slide after the last one? If unchecked, stops at last slide.

small_screen_optimized.name: Optimization for Joan 6"
small_screen_optimized.description: Check this if using a 6-inch device (e.g., Joan 6).

action_webhook_id.name: Webhook ID (Right Button) (→)
action_webhook_id.description: ID of the webhook triggered when pressing the right button (arrow).

action_webhook_2_id.name: Webhook ID (Center Button) (✓)
action_webhook_2_id.description: ID of the webhook triggered when pressing the center button (check).

click_anywhere_to_action.name: Click Triggers Action
click_anywhere_to_action.description: If checked, hides other buttons. Touching ANY part of the screen will trigger the Right Button Webhook (→).

add_back_button.name: Show Back Button on Left (←)
add_back_button.description: Adds an exit/back button. For each device, configure the target in Views Configuration (Visionect Joan).

click_anywhere_to_return.name: Click Triggers Back
click_anywhere_to_return.description: If checked, hides other buttons. Touching ANY part of the screen will trigger the Left Back Button (←).

back_button_url.name: Override Back Button Target
back_button_url.description: Highest priority. If you provide a URL or View Name here, the Back Button (←) will navigate there.

auto_return_seconds.name: Auto Return Back (seconds)
auto_return_seconds.description: Automatically closes the panel after time. Returns to the return target configured in device options.

set_session_options.name: Set Session Options
set_session_options.description: Advanced configuration of how server processes image for the device.

encoding.name: Encoding (Bit Depth)
encoding.description: Select 4-bit for gray shades (nicer image) or 1-bit for pure black and white (faster refresh).

dithering.name: Dithering Method
dithering.description: Algorithm simulating gray shades with dots. Affects photo quality.

send_keypad.name: Send PIN Keypad
send_keypad.description: Displays screen with numeric keypad. User can enter code which is sent to Home Assistant. Requires a Webhook automation to be configured. The automation trigger must use this ID. The entered code will be available in the template as trigger.json.pin. Example condition: trigger.json.pin == 1234.

title.name: Screen Title
title.description: Prompt text, e.g., Enter Alarm Code.

action_webhook_id.name: Receiver Webhook ID
action_webhook_id.description: The ID of the webhook automation that will receive the PIN. The trigger must use this ID. Entered code available as trigger.json.pin.

auto_return_seconds.name: Auto Return Back (seconds)
auto_return_seconds.description: Time to enter code before auto-close.

send_button_panel.name: Send Button Panel
send_button_panel.description: Displays configurable grid of buttons (up to 12). Each button can trigger a different action in Home Assistant. Requires Webhook automation, set button does not show current HA state.

title.name: Panel Title
title.description: Header visible at top of page.

small_screen_optimized.name: Optimization for Joan 6"
small_screen_optimized.description: Check this if using a 6-inch device (e.g., Joan 6).

add_back_button.name: Show Back Button on Left (←)
add_back_button.description: Adds an exit/back button. For each device, configure the target in Views Configuration (Visionect Joan).

click_anywhere_to_return.name: Click Triggers Back
click_anywhere_to_return.description: If checked, hides other buttons. Touching ANY part of the screen will trigger the Left Back Button (←).

back_button_url.name: Override Back Button Target
back_button_url.description: Highest priority. If you provide a URL or View Name here, the Back Button (←) will navigate there.

action_webhook_id.name: Webhook ID (Right Button) (→)
action_webhook_id.description: ID of the webhook triggered when pressing the right button (arrow).

action_webhook_2_id.name: Webhook ID (Center Button) (✓)
action_webhook_2_id.description: ID of the webhook triggered when pressing the center button (check).

auto_return_seconds.name: Auto Return Back (seconds)
auto_return_seconds.description: Automatically closes the panel after time. Returns to the return target configured in device options.

click_anywhere_to_action.name: Click Triggers Action
click_anywhere_to_action.description: If checked, hides other buttons. Touching ANY part of the screen will trigger the Right Button Webhook (→).

read_safe_device_config.name: Safe Config — Schritt 1/3: TCLV lesen
read_safe_device_config.description: Schritt 1/3 (optional, Diagnose): liest TCLV-Liste sowie Heart Beat, Systembildschirme und Touch über VSS. Manche Firmware hat kein Live-Readback; nach Schritt 2 ggf. Cache-Werte. Üblicherweise danach Schritt 2 (apply).

device_id.name: Gerät
device_id.description: Visionect-Gerät, für das die sicheren TCLV-Felder gelesen werden.

apply_safe_device_config.name: Safe Config — Schritt 2/3: TCLV schreiben
apply_safe_device_config.description: Schritt 2/3 (eigentliche Änderung): schreibt Heart Beat, Systembildschirme und Touch-Modus per TCLV. Legt vor dem Schreiben ein Backup der bisherigen Werte an (für Schritt 3). Schritt 1 (Lesen) ist optional. Standard Heart Beat: 3 Min.

heart_beat_interval.name: Heart Beat Interval
heart_beat_interval.description: How often (in minutes) the tablet sends heartbeat/status pings to server. Default: 3 min.

system_screens.name: System Screens
system_screens.description: Firmware system screens (TCLV 49). Defines what tablet shows on low battery / disconnected state.

touch_mode.name: Touch Mode
touch_mode.description: Touch mode (TCLV 50). VSS-compatible values: 0 = Off, 1 = On, 3 = On + Beep.

restore_safe_device_config.name: Safe Config — Schritt 3/3: Backup wiederherstellen
restore_safe_device_config.description: Schritt 3/3 (optional): stellt TCLV-Werte aus dem Backup wieder her, das unmittelbar vor dem letzten Apply auf diesem Gerät angelegt wurde. Ohne vorherigen Apply gibt es kein Backup — zuerst Schritt 2.

device_id.name: Gerät
device_id.description: Dasselbe Gerät wie bei apply_safe_device_config; stellt dessen gespeichertes Backup wieder her.

send_crypto.name: Send Crypto Panel
send_crypto.description: Displays a cryptocurrency panel with prices, trends and optional sparkline history.

coins.name: Coins
coins.description: List of coins to display, e.g. bitcoin, ethereum.

vs_currency.name: Display Currency
vs_currency.description: Currency used for prices, e.g. usd, eur, pln.

history_hours.name: Sparkline History (hours)
history_hours.description: How many hours of history to display in the sparkline. Set 0 to disable.

show_header.name: Show Header
show_header.description: Show or hide the panel title/header.
